# Goal: TUI v2.0 — Three-Pane Glass Cockpit

_Target: `g_20260418_103214_db0261` · task `t_20260418_110439_d2b77f` · locked_hash `a73efcacf0b470c1`_

## Purpose

`src/agentboard/tui/` (510 LOC, Textual) was declared observability-only and offered no command surface (source: plan.md ## Problem). Users could not explore goals, runs, decisions, or learnings, and the two hidden hotkeys (Ctrl+P pause / Ctrl+I hint) depended on the LEGACY `orchestrator.interrupt.HintQueue` which is dead under the current MCP-driven loop (source: plan.md ## Problem). Net effect: users perceived the TUI as "poor" because there was nothing to do in it — the cockpit existed but had no instruments.

## Plan

Scope was held (no expansion) and a three-pane Textual layout was locked in: `Header(HealthBar) + Horizontal(ResourcesView 20% | LiveStreamView 45% | ContextViewer 35%) + CommandLine bottom-dock + Footer` (source: plan.md ## Architecture). Read-only data flow — `FileStore.load_*` seeds initial state; a `TailWorker` running under `@work(thread=True)` polls `runs/*.jsonl` + `decisions.jsonl` every 100 ms via `os.stat()` size-delta, parses incremental lines, feeds an `AnomalyClassifier`, and surfaces them via `call_from_thread → LiveStreamView.append + HealthBar.flash` (source: plan.md ## Architecture). A `:`-triggered `CommandLine` dispatches six commands (`:goals :runs :diff :decisions :goto :learn`) through a `CommandRegistry`. Scope bounds: token_ceiling 150000, max_iterations 7, 16 checklist items broken into 25 atomic TDD steps (source: plan.md ## Budget).

## Process

Engineering review passed on iter 0 — architecture single-responsibility, no abstraction cycles, out_of_scope_guard enumerated (source: decisions.jsonl iter=0 eng_review PASS). Seven TDD green cycles followed, landing iter 7 review PASS with 369 tests green (source: iter=7 review). Then redteam round 1 (iter 8) returned BROKEN: `:goals foo` crashed `CommandRegistry.dispatch` with TypeError, `AnomalyClassifier` died on non-dict records, and `FileTail` raised `UnicodeDecodeError` when a multi-byte UTF-8 char split across polls (source: iter=8 redteam). Round 2 (iter 9) returned BROKEN with a CRITICAL: the App never actually wired `TailWorker → LiveStreamView` — unit tests passed because primitives were called directly, but `agentboard board` showed zero live events (source: iter=9 redteam). Round 3 (iter 10) returned BROKEN again: `LiveStreamView`'s body `Static` had `markup=False`, so color tags rendered as literal `[red]...[/]` bracket text — goal_checklist "4 anomalies colored" was silently violated, the substring test had been fooled (source: iter=10 redteam). Each round added failing RED tests before the fix; reviews after 8/9/10 all came back PASS with re-verified 16/16 checklist (source: iter=8, 9, 10 review). Round 4 (iter 11) returned SURVIVED — only MEDIUM/HIGH items remained (runs TOCTOU, missing batch cap, HealthBar timer stampede) with no crash path (source: iter=11 redteam).

## Result

Final state on merge: 379 tests green (345 baseline + 24 TUI + 10 redteam regression), 16/16 goal_checklist items carrying pytest evidence via `agentboard_verify`, 34 TUI tests total (source: iter=10 review). The legacy `from agentboard.orchestrator.interrupt import get_hint_queue` import was removed from the new `app.py`; `app_legacy.py` + `test_tui.py` retain the old behavior during migration (source: iter=7 review). A single dependency — `rapidfuzz` — was added per the `?` help fuzzy-search borderline resolution (source: iter=7 review). 2026-04-18 22:38 UTC: direct push to `origin/main` as commit `33543cd`, squash policy collapsed six iter commits into one clean commit, no PR (direct-to-main workflow) (source: iter=11 approval).

## Review

The four redteam rounds revealed a structural weakness in spec-literal TDD: checklist strings paired with unit tests that exercise primitives in isolation will certify a cockpit whose main App never wires its own workers — this was the CRITICAL finding of round 2 (source: iter=9 redteam). Framework footguns (Textual `Static(markup=False)` swallowing color tags, `FileTail` byte-slicing multibyte chars) are invisible to unit tests and only surface when the render pipeline or a real non-ASCII tail runs end-to-end (source: iter=8, 10 redteam). The `LiveStreamView`'s unbounded `Static` O(n²) growth was accepted as a known-risk deferral, logged rather than fixed (source: iter=10 review). These rounds produced two of the highest-confidence learnings in `.agentboard/learnings/`: `unit-tests-on-primitives-dont-prove-integration` (conf 0.95) and `textual-static-markup-flag-silently-breaks-color` (conf 0.95), both of which now appear in the Preemptive Defense Checklist of subsequent TUI tasks — including this very audit's RED step (source: iter=8, 10 redteam; .agentboard/learnings index).
